violations.text: A rodent trap was observed with a dead mouse caught in it. PIC is to ensure the traps set as part of current pest control program are routinely checked/monitored for any dead rodents. Dead rodents and other pests shall be removed from control devices and the PREMISES at a frequency that prevents their accumulation, decomposition, or the attraction of pests. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

Single service items in the basement area were observed are not being stored properly in protective packaging. PIC is to ensure single-use items used for food are protected from possible contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The non-food-contact surfaces of the cookline equipment (hood screens, sides of fryolators, shelving below fryolators, and the grease pump machine) were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The following areas of the physical facilities were observed in need of more frequent routine cleaning... - The wall(s) and floor under/behind equipment, in the back prep, cooking, and cookline refrigeration areas - The floor under and behind equipment in the basement area These areas were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and old droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. (Employees began cleaning at the time of inspection.) New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, deli slicer, potato slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Equipment was taken out of service for proper cleaning at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2025

The non-food-contact surfaces of the refrigeration, shelving, and clean knife holder, were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2025

The wall(s), ceiling, and floor under/behind equipment, in the back prep and cooking areas were observed to have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2025

The wall attachments (Moldings) and built-in shelving were observed to have worn sealed surface exposing wood making it no longer smooth, durable, easily cleanable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (Potato slicer, deli slicer, ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. All equipment was taken out of service. Equipment must be washed rinsed and sanitized prior to use. Ice was voluntarily disposed of. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2023

Meatballs, which was previously cooked and cooled, were only reheated to 114 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food that is cooked, cooled, and reheated for hot holding must be reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. Food was moved to stove top to be rapidly reheated. Proper reheating procedures reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2023

The grease trap outside is located on gravel. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2023

Behind equipment in the kitchen has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. Old rodent droppings were observed in the basement area. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2023
